segments.title: National roles shape destroyer design
segments.summary: A destroyer optimized for fleet protection, offensive attack, or general-purpose duties will make different tradeoffs in guns, torpedoes, anti-submarine warfare, and minelaying.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke argues that no destroyer is ideal in the abstract: its best features depend on the national role it is expected to perform. A navy treating destroyers chiefly as a shield may favour gun firepower, while an offensive force may stress torpedoes; a general-purpose design also needs anti-submarine and minelaying capability. The comparison frames destroyer design as a balance of operational priorities rather than a universal checklist.

evidence.note: The transcript contrasts shield, spear, and general-purpose destroyer roles with their armament and capability priorities.
